title: Pasta e Ceci

overview.description: A traditional Italian pasta and chickpea soup that is hearty, economical and made almost entirely from pantry ingredients. Thick enough to be a meal on its own and perfect with homemade bread.

ingredients: 2 × 400g tins chickpeas, drained (reserve the liquid if desired)
150g small pasta (ditalini, small shells or macaroni)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
Extra virgin olive oil, to serve
method: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over a medium heat. Add the onion, carrot and celery and cook until softened, about 10 minutes.
Add the garlic, tomato purée, oregano and chilli flakes (if using). Cook for 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the chickpeas and stock, then bring to a gentle simmer.
Blend about one third of the soup until smooth, then return it to the pan. This creates a naturally creamy texture without adding cream.
Add the pasta and cook until just tender, stirring occasionally to prevent it sticking.
Season to taste with salt and black pepper.
Serve topped with grated Parmesan, chopped parsley and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.

learning.did-you-know: Pasta e Ceci has been enjoyed across Italy for centuries and demonstrates how simple pantry ingredients can become an incredibly satisfying meal.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How soffritto forms the flavour base of many Italian dishes, how blending part of a soup naturally thickens it, and how to cook pasta directly in soup.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A perfect Pantry Monday meal that's inexpensive, filling, nutritious and made almost entirely from cupboard staples.
chapman-tips: Blend only part of the soup to keep plenty of texture.
Cook the pasta until just al dente as it will continue softening in the hot soup.
A drizzle of good olive oil before serving makes a noticeable difference.
notes.batch-cooking: The soup freezes well without the pasta. If freezing, cook and add fresh pasta when reheating for the best texture.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently with a splash of stock or water, as the pasta will absorb liquid overnight.
